data.scenario:
Пятница 8 AM. Флот 4 трака:
• Truck 1: Reefer — frozen meat, delivery today 2 PM in Houston. Driver says reefer temp alarm went off (temp: 10°F, should be -5°F).
• Truck 2: Flatbed — oversize transformer, en route to Denver. Driver just got speeding ticket (72 in 55 zone).
• Truck 3: Empty in Newark, NJ — no loads found for 2 days (slow market).
• Truck 4: Loaded, delivery complete at 7 AM — waiting for your next instruction in Atlanta.
Что ваш ПЕРВЫЙ action?

options.text: Truck 3 — он пустой 2 дня, нужен груз

Truck 1 — REEFER ALARM = cargo at immediate risk. Temp 10°F vs required -5°F = rising fast. Action: have driver check reefer unit (fuel? settings? mechanical?). If simple fix (out of fuel) = refuel immediately. If mechanical = find nearest reefer service. 15°F gap = 2-3 hours before cargo is compromised. TIME CRITICAL.
Truck 4 — он свободен, нужно найти груз пока рано

data.problem: Рассчитайте fleet utilization rate и revenue per truck per day.
data.context:
Fleet: 4 trucks, 5 working days.
Truck 1: worked 5/5 days, revenue $5,200.
Truck 2: worked 4/5 days (1 day breakdown), revenue $3,600.
Truck 3: worked 3/5 days (2 days empty), revenue $2,800.
Truck 4: worked 5/5 days, revenue $4,900.
Utilization = total working days ÷ total available days × 100.
Then calculate average revenue per truck per WORKING day (total revenue ÷ total working days).
Вам нужно закрыть 2 трака одновременно. Truck A пустой в Memphis (reefer). Truck B пустой в Nashville (dry van). Broker из CH Robinson имеет оба типа грузов. Вы хотите bundle deal.

turns.speaker: Брокер
turns.message: I have two loads. Reefer: Memphis to Miami, 1,000 miles, frozen seafood, $2,800. Dry van: Nashville to Jacksonville, 630 miles, auto parts, $1,500. Both pickup tomorrow. What can you do for me?

replies.text: I can cover BOTH loads — that saves you two phone calls and two carriers to manage. Here's my pricing: Reefer Memphis-Miami: $3,400 — it's frozen seafood (high liability), 1,000 miles, and reefer fuel adds cost. Dry van Nash-Jax: $1,850 — current market for that lane is $2.85+/mile. Bundle both: I'll do $5,100 total ($3,300 + $1,800). You get one carrier, one point of contact, reliable coverage. Deal?
replies.feedback: Master-level negotiation! Вы: 1) Bundle leverage — 'saves you two phone calls'. 2) Justified each rate with logic (reefer liability, market data). 3) Offered slight bundle discount ($5,250 → $5,100). 4) Value proposition: one carrier = simplicity for broker. This is how top dispatchers work: volume deals, relationship building, both sides win.

replies.text: I'll take both at your posted rates. $2,800 and $1,500. Send Rate Cons.
replies.feedback: Вы потеряли $1,200-1,500! Reefer frozen seafood at $2.80/mile = below market (reefer + perishable = $3.20-3.80/mile). Dry van at $2.38/mile = borderline for Nashville outbound. You have LEVERAGE: offering both trucks = convenience for broker. Use it! Negotiate UP.

replies.text: Those rates are insulting. I need $4,000 for the reefer and $2,500 for the dry van. Take it or leave it.
replies.feedback: $4.00/mile reefer Memphis→Miami = above market even for peak. $3.97/mile dry van Nashville→Jacksonville = unrealistic. Broker will laugh and move on. Know your market: reefer FL-bound $3.20-3.60/mile. Dry van SE corridor: $2.60-3.00/mile. Price within reason + bundle discount = closes deals.

turns.speaker: Брокер
turns.message: Look, $5,100 for both is more than my budget. Best I can do is $4,600 total. Final offer — I have other carriers calling me.

replies.text: Let me meet you halfway. $4,850 total — I'll split it $3,100 reefer + $1,750 dry van. That's my floor. Both trucks are ready, clean inspections, experienced drivers. I can confirm right now. Otherwise, you're making 2 more calls and risking coverage on a Friday for tomorrow pickup.
replies.feedback: Отличный close! Вы: 1) Compromised ($5,100 → $4,850 = $250 concession). 2) Split allocation логично. 3) Urgency reminder: Friday + tomorrow pickup = broker pressed for time. 4) 'Confirm right now' = immediate value. $4,850 vs original $4,300 = +$550 negotiated. That's real money. Professional.

Вы jumped too fast! Broker said '$4,600 final' — classic negotiation tactic. NEVER accept 'final offer' immediately. Counter $4,800-4,900. Even $100 more = money in your pocket. 'Final offer' rarely is final when you have leverage (2 trucks, Friday, tomorrow pickup). Test it.

replies.text: $5,100 is my price. If you can't pay it, goodbye.
replies.feedback: Rigidity = lost deal. $5,100 → $4,600 gap = $500. Neither side will get exactly what they want. Smart negotiators find middle ground. You leave with $4,850 (won $550 vs posted), broker saves vs your ask. Both trucks loaded, both making money. Walking away = two empty trucks on Monday.

data.subject: Scheduled Compliance Review — MC-892XXX — Documents Required
data.body:
Dear Carrier,

Your compliance review is scheduled for March 15.

Please have the following available:

1. ELD records — all drivers, past 6 months
2. Driver Qualification Files (DQ files) — all active drivers
3. Drug & Alcohol testing records (random pool participation proof)
4. Vehicle maintenance records & DVIR logs
5. Insurance certificates (current)
6. Accident register (last 3 years)
7. Hours of Service supporting documents

Review will take approximately 4-6 hours on-site.

Auditor: James Mitchell
FMCSA Region 4

responses.text: Ответить: 'Mr. Mitchell, confirmed for March 15. All documents will be prepared.' Затем внутренний action plan: 1) Export 6-month ELD records from Samsara. 2) Audit DQ files: verify each driver has current CDL copy, medical card (not expired!), MVR, application, road test cert. 3) Confirm drug test consortium — get participation letter. 4) Compile maintenance records + DVIR downloads. 5) Verify insurance certificates current. 6) Prepare accident register. 7) Self-audit: find and fix gaps BEFORE auditor arrives.
responses.feedback: Подготовка = survival! Most common audit failures: 1) Expired medical cards in DQ file (driver disqualified + violation). 2) Missing random drug test records. 3) Gaps in ELD data. 4) No maintenance records for repairs. Self-audit week before = find issues, fix them, document corrections. Auditor sees organized carrier with corrective actions = Satisfactory rating. Messy files = Conditional or Unsatisfactory.

responses.text: Попросить перенести аудит на более позднюю дату
responses.feedback: FMCSA compliance review = MANDATORY. Вы можете попросить reschedule один раз (valid reason only), но это выглядит suspicious и auditor приедет более thorough. Лучше: принять дату и ПОДГОТОВИТЬСЯ идеально. 3+ weeks notice = достаточно времени собрать ВСЕ документы. Delay = red flag для FMCSA.

Consultant не плохая идея для first-time audit, но: 1) Стоит $2,000-5,000. 2) ВЫ всё равно должны иметь документы. 3) Consultant не может создать records that don't exist. Лучший вариант: подготовьтесь САМИ (это ваша работа как dispatcher/safety manager), и consultant только для final review если budget позволяет.

data.question: Ваш carrier получил Conditional Safety Rating. Какой КАСКАД последствий это вызовет?
options: Insurance premium +30-50% при renewal. Brokers с strict compliance = отказ работать. Contract shippers (Walmart, Amazon) = terminated. Spot market = reduced opportunities. Bank loan = risk. 60 days to fix or face Unsatisfactory → shutdown.
data.explanation: Conditional = домино эффект: 1) Insurance: instant 30-50% increase (or non-renewal). 2) Shippers: Fortune 500 companies auto-terminate Conditional carriers. 3) Brokers: many won't book (TQL, CH Robinson have thresholds). 4) Revenue: -30-50% from lost lanes/clients. 5) Banking: loan terms may change. 6) Recruitment: good drivers don't want Conditional carriers. 7) Clock ticking: 60 days. Это financial DEATH SPIRAL если не исправить быстро.

tasks.title: Final scenario: ethical dilemma under pressure

data.scenario: Конец месяца. Ваш fleet P&L показывает $4,000 loss. Один O/O предлагает: 'Я могу сделать extra load если поеду без 10-hr break (нарушу HOS). Никто не узнает — я выключу ELD на 2 часа. Мы заработаем $2,200.' Другой driver предлагает: 'Я могу перевезти overweight load (84,000 lbs вместо 80,000 max) — shipper платит extra $800.' Оба нарушения могут 'спасти' месяц.

options.text: Согласиться на оба — бизнес есть бизнес, нужно платить bills

ОТКАЗАТЬ ОБОИМ. HOS violation: $16,000 fine + CSA points + если авария while fatigued = criminal charges для ВАС как dispatcher. Overweight: $1,000-10,000 fine при weigh station + bridge damage liability. $3,000 'saved' today = $50,000+ risk. Найти ЛЕГАЛЬНЫЕ решения: negotiate better rates, add loads, cut costs. Never compromise safety for short-term cash.
Согласиться только на overweight — это менее опасно
Согласиться только на HOS — один раз не страшно

data.problem: Рассчитайте месячный NET PROFIT carrier с учётом ВСЕХ расходов.
data.context:
Monthly (3 trucks):
Gross Revenue: $48,000.
Expenses:
- Fuel: 3 trucks × 9,000 mi × (1/6.1 mpg) × $3.85 = ?
- Insurance: $1,950/month
- Truck payments: 3 × $1,800 = $5,400
- Maintenance reserve: $1,200
- Dispatcher salary: $5,200
- ELD/Software: $135/month
- Permits/registrations: $250/month
- Office/phone: $350/month
Calculate total fuel cost first (3 trucks × 9000mi ÷ 6.1mpg × $3.85), then subtract ALL expenses from revenue.

pairs.definition: % времени когда траки загружены и зарабатывают
Рейтинг safety vs other carriers (ниже = лучше)
Баланс между volatile spot и stable contract freight

data.context: Ваш годовой contract с P&G истекает. Они предлагают renewal по старой ставке ($2.65/mile). За год: diesel вырос на $0.40/gal, insurance +20%, driver pay +$0.08/mile. Вы должны negotiate increase.

turns.speaker: P&G Representative
turns.message: Hi, we'd like to renew our Cincinnati-Atlanta lane contract. Same terms as last year: $2.65/mile, 3-4 loads per week. You've been reliable all year — we appreciate it. Can we sign for another 12 months?

replies.text: Thank you — we value the P&G partnership. However, our operating costs have increased significantly since last year: diesel +$0.40/gallon (+$0.10/mile effective), insurance +20%, driver compensation +$0.08/mile. To maintain service quality, we need $2.95/mile for the new term. That's a 11% increase matching our cost inflation. I'd also like to add a fuel surcharge clause (DOE-indexed) to protect both sides from future volatility. Thoughts?
replies.feedback: Профессиональный renewal negotiation! Вы: 1) Started positive (value relationship). 2) Quantified EACH cost increase (not vague 'costs went up'). 3) Specific number: $2.95 (not 'more money'). 4) FSC clause = forward protection. 5) Framed as partnership ('protect both sides'). $2.95 × 460mi × ~14 loads/month = $19,054/month vs old $17,122 = +$1,932/month. FSC adds further protection.

replies.text: Sure, $2.65 is fine for another year. Let's sign.
replies.feedback: Ваши costs grew 10-15% но revenue stays flat! $2.65/mile with $0.18/mile cost increase = effective rate drop to $2.47/mile value. За 12 месяцев: $0.18 × 460mi × 168 loads = $13,910 LOST to inflation. Каждый year вы MUST negotiate rate increases matching (or exceeding) cost increases. Stagnant rate = shrinking profit.

replies.text: We need $3.50/mile or we walk away from this contract.
replies.feedback: $3.50 = 32% increase. P&G will say no and find another carrier. Rate increases of 10-15% are reasonable and justifiable. 30%+ requires extraordinary circumstances (market crisis, supply shortage). Don't blow up a reliable contract for unrealistic demands — $2.95-3.05 is achievable and fair.

data.sentence: Multi-truck fleet: приоритет = {0} emergencies first. O/O dispatch fee стандарт: {1}%. Produce season начинается в {2}. CSA threshold для intervention: {3} percentile. Rate Con ВСЕГДА проверяйте наличие {4} clause.
